summary: Chromium-family browser cookie and saved-password stealer with bytenode-obfuscated payload and native Windows addon
details: Package @ibrahim1337/baksen@2.1.1 ships a Chromium-family infostealer disguised as a generic Windows-only npm module. The main entry loads a bytenode-compiled bytecode blob (index.jsc) plus a 729KB native Windows addon at build/Release/debugelevator.node. On execution, the code locates Chrome, Brave, Edge, Opera, and Opera GX user-data directories, terminates running browser processes via `taskkill /F /IM`, copies the encrypted Cookies and Login Data SQLite databases to temp, extracts the DPAPI master key and the Chrome v20 App-Bound encryption key (`app_bound_encrypted_key`), then AES-256-GCM-decrypts every cookie (host_key/name/value, including live session cookies usable for account takeover) and every saved login (origin_url/username_value/password_value). Cleartext credentials are written to./<browser>_cookies/ and./<browser>_passwords/. A separate license module (src/license.jsc) fetches an allowlist of license keys from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tabo1337/SaatPCBKod/refs/heads/main/key.txt — a mutable branch on a third-party GitHub account (tabo1337) distinct from the npm publisher (ibrahim1337) — and gates execution on the fetched list, giving the remote operator a runtime kill/enable switch. Implementation is concealed via bytenode bytecode compilation and js-confuser obfuscation. Publisher metadata is placeholder (repository URL `https://github.com/yourusername/baksen`, description `hadiyapic`), os/cpu pinned to win32/x64. This is a credential-theft tool packaged as an npm module.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
